matches.rationale: The extracted rule is a Bash rule triggered before pull-request creation after library changes, and its name and evidence encompass the required make format command. Under the 1:1 constraint it is assigned to this single gold rule.
The Edit rule directly detects Sphinx-style double-backtick content in Python edits, matching the prohibition on adding or changing such inline references.

misses.note: The lint requirement is a distinct item in a three-item Markdown list. The compiler collapsed that list into one combined extracted rule, which can only be assigned once under the 1:1 rule.
The test requirement is a distinct item in a three-item Markdown list. The compiler collapsed that list into one combined extracted rule, which can only be assigned once under the 1:1 rule.
The compiler deliberately skipped this usage rule as uncompilable: "TEST 변수로 특정 pytest 파일/옵션을 지정하는 사용법 안내는 강제 규칙이 아닌 CLI 사용법 문서라 스킵".

The positive single-backtick requirement is explicit, but the compiler combined it with the double-backtick prohibition in one extracted rule. That rule is closer to g5 because its pattern specifically detects double backticks and cannot also match g6 under the 1:1 constraint.
